channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: West Coast Eagles secure convincing 30-point win over Essendon as rebuild shows progress
item.description: West Coast produced one of their most convincing performances of the 2026 AFL season, defeating Essendon by 30 points at Optus Stadium and continuing to show signs of meaningful progress in their rebu...

item.title: Pakistan reports limited progress in negotiations with Chinese power producers over tariff discounts
item.description: Power Minister Awais Leghari has said that Pakistan has not yet achieved meaningful progress in negotiations with Chinese power producers to secure tariff discounts under CPEC-linked power projects. H...

item.title: City of Johannesburg and National Treasury clash over R10.3bn municipal wage agreement amid funding threat
item.description: The City of Johannesburg is locked in a growing standoff with South Africa’s National Treasury over a controversial R10.3bn wage agreement signed with municipal workers. The deal, concluded last year ...

item.title: Bengal Governor Warns Against Undermining Public Trust After Election Results
item.description: West Bengal Governor R N Ravi, during an event in north Bengal, made remarks indirectly targeting the Trinamool Congress (TMC), cautioning that questioning democratic institutions after electoral loss...

item.title: Former ED investigator in high-profile Nirav Modi and Vijay Mallya cases takes voluntary retirement
item.description: Satyabrata Kumar, a senior Indian Revenue Service (IRS) officer and former Special Director of the Enforcement Directorate (ED), has opted for voluntary retirement from service (VRS), according to off...

item.title: Tanzanian Musician Malisa Reflects on Life Lessons Learned During 183 Days in Prison
item.description: Tanzanian musician Malisa recently opened up about her experience spending 183 days in prison, sharing both the emotional and physical challenges she faced. She described the harsh realities of prison...

item.title: Aranyak and the Early Literary Vision of Ecological Loss and Moral Conflict
item.description: The article examines Bibhutibhushan Bandyopadhyay’s 1939 Bengali novel Aranyak as a remarkably early literary exploration of ecological destruction and its emotional consequences. Set in the forests o...

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Moss & Fog: Wildlife, Nature Blooms, and Art Installations (May 25–31, 2026)
item.description: This weekly edition of Moss & Fog’s “Fog Signals” curates a selection of visually striking and unusual stories spanning nature, wildlife, and contemporary art. The issue highlights the aardwolf, a noc...

item.title: Rob Schneider uses 'Waterboy' character on Fox News to support Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt
item.description: Comedian Rob Schneider made a politically charged appearance on Fox News on May 31, 2026, where he endorsed Republican mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt while invoking a well-known character from his fi...

item.title: UDSM and Iranian Cultural Centre Promote Arts Education and Cultural Exchange for Youth Empowerment
item.description: Young people in Tanzania have been encouraged to take arts education seriously as a pathway to employment, cultural diplomacy, and preservation of cultural heritage following a collaborative programme...

item.title: CATA taxi association president arrested ahead of court appearance in Paarl
item.description: The National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) has confirmed the arrest of Siviwe Kiva, the president of the Cape Amalgamated Taxi Association (CATA), who is expected to appear in the Paarl Magistrate’s Cou...

item.title: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger Faces Backlash Over Vetoes
item.description: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger has sparked controversy within her own Democratic Party by vetoing 31 bills passed by the Democratic-led legislature, actions she considers moderate. The vetoes in...

item.title: One reported dead in bus and coaster collision in Mkuranga district
item.description: A road accident involving a bus and a coaster minibus occurred in Mkuranga district in the Pwani Region, about 35 kilometers south of Dar es Salaam. According to local authorities, the crash took plac...

item.title: Olawepo-Hashim Confirmed as Accord Party Presidential Candidate After Nationwide Affirmation Process
item.description: Dr. Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim has officially secured the presidential ticket of the Accord Party following the conclusion of the party’s affirmation primaries conducted across several states in Nigeria. T...

item.title: Trump challenges judge over Kennedy Center renovation and naming dispute
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump has called for the impeachment of a federal judge after a ruling temporarily halted his plans to renovate the Kennedy Center and add his name to the building. The de...

item.title: U.S. military strike in eastern Pacific kills three amid ongoing anti-drug trafficking campaign
item.description: A U.S. military strike in the eastern Pacific Ocean has killed three men aboard a vessel that American authorities allege was involved in drug smuggling operations. The U.S. Southern Command announced...

item.title: Court of Appeal rules in favour of Diamond Trust Bank in disputed matrimonial property loan case
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Kenya has ruled in favour of Diamond Trust Bank (DTB) in a long-running dispute involving a matrimonial home used as collateral for a loan. The case was brought by Fatma Hadi, w...

item.title: The Darkness release ‘Eternia’ as the official theme song for the upcoming Masters Of The Universe film
item.description: British rock band The Darkness have unveiled ‘Eternia’, an 80s-inspired anthem created for the forthcoming Masters Of The Universe film, which is scheduled for release in cinemas on 5 June. The movie ...

item.title: Former Trump campaign adviser warns of potential wave of congressional subpoenas if House control shifts
item.description: David Urban, a Republican strategist who previously served as senior campaign adviser to Donald Trump during the 2016 election, issued a stark warning about the political consequences the president co...

item.title: Power minister confirms disciplinary action against LESCO official following corruption complaint raised by Khawaja Asif
item.description: Pakistan’s Power Minister Awais Leghari has confirmed that strict disciplinary action has been taken against a Lahore Electric Supply Company (LESCO) line superintendent following corruption allegatio...
